data.title: Indonesia Stock Exchange CEO resigns, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
The head of Indonesia's stock exchange has resigned, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

Indonesian stocks have been roiled this week after index provider MSCI said the market risked a downgrade to frontier status on concerns about trading and ownership transparency.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Could Benefit From Positive Liquidity Inflows Trends — Market Talk
data.description: Bursa Malaysia could be a key beneficiary of the positive liquidity inflow trends, RHB IB analyst David Chong says in a note. Securities' average daily value has got off to a strong start, with year-to-date turnover at MYR3.3 billion, he says. Bursa has said that MSCI's recent warning about Indonesian securities' investability is specific to Indonesia and doesn't raise similar concerns for Malaysia, he notes. While Bursa doesn't rule out the possibility of capturing some liquidity diverted from Indonesia, its priority is to enhance the attractiveness and vibrancy of the local market. RHB upgrades Bursa's rating to buy from neutral and raises the target price to MYR9.95 from MYR8.10. Shares are 0.4% higher at MYR9.06. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Under Pressure, Still Head for Solid Monthly Gain
data.description:
Shares in Hong Kong plunged 412 points, or 1.5%, to 27,545 in early Friday trade, snapping a seven-session winning streak amid widespread losses across sectors.

Traders secured profits after markets hit a 4-1/2-year high in the prior session, while caution mounted ahead of China’s official January PMI data due over the weekend.

Sentiment was also pressured by a sharp drop in U.S. stock futures, following a mostly lower close on Wall Street overnight after Apple warned of margin headwinds.

Zijin Gold Intl. fell 6.9%, followed by Zhaojin Mining (-4.1%), Kuaishou Tech. (-2.9%), and Li Auto (-2.4%).

Still, Hong Kong equities remain on track for a third straight weekly gain and are poised to log their first monthly rise in four months, up near 8% so far.

These were lifted by signs of stabilization in the city’s property market.

In China, authorities reportedly scrapped requirements for builders to submit monthly data linked to debt ratio caps, easing financing constraints imposed in 2020.

data.title: Gold's Continued Surge May Affect Investors' Perception of Fiat Currencies — Market Talk
data.description:
2120 ET - A continued surge in gold and other previous metals could lead to volatilities in stock and bond markets, says CGS International's Bertram Lai in a note. Gold and other precious metals appear to be in a self-reinforcing feedback loop as their price action has become the news driving their own prices, he says. This is likely to influence investors' perception of risk associated with fiat currencies, he says. That could potentially result in a larger bond risk premium at the longer-end of the yield curve, he says. "If the bond risk premium materially re-prices over the coming months, that might contribute to a major episode of volatility in equity and credit markets," he adds. (megan.cheah@wsj.com)

2056 ET - South Korea is set to post strong export growth in January due to brisk semiconductor shipments and more working days. The median forecast of nine economists polled by The Wall Street Journal is for exports to have increased 30% on year, compared with a revised 13.3% rise in December. Imports are projected to have risen 12% on year, resulting in a $6.30 billion trade surplus in January, compared with a revised $12.17 billion surplus the prior month, the poll shows. Analysts say January exports likely got a boost from solid chip demand as well as from 3.5 more working days due to a shift of Lunar New Year holidays to mid-February this year. The data are due Sunday. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

2030 ET - The Philippine central bank is highly likely to cut its rate in February, as 4Q GDP growth slowdown was sharper-than-expected, Citi Research economists write in a note. Domestic demand continues to be dragged by a corruption scandal, relating to the alleged misuse of public funds for state flood-management initiatives. Citi doesn't rule out an additional rate cut in April, if 1Q GDP growth continues to decelerates. However, global oil prices will also need to be closely monitored, as any surge in prices could raise Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas' concern over imported inflation. "This may adversely impact the possibility of any further cut in April," Citi says.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

1930 ET - Japan's Nikkei Stock Average declines 0.2% to 53256.62 as falls in some electronics stocks help offset gains in pharmaceutical and energy shares. Advantest drops 5.3% and Keyence is 3.8% lower while Chugai Pharmaceutical is up 4.4% and Eneos Holdings is 3.0% higher. Broader market index Topix rises 0.5% to 3561.66. USD/JPY is at 153.39, compared with 152.96 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching earnings, with Daiichi Sankyo, Hoya and Komatsu scheduled to announce their results. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's economic plans are also in focus ahead of the general election early next month. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

1929 ET - Asian currencies consolidate against the dollar in morning trade but may be weighed by the ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions. There are expectations of a weekend strike on Iran, IG's Chris Beauchamp says in an email. "Evidence suggests the U.S. will go for a major and extended campaign, which inevitably raises fears that Tehran will retaliate," the chief market analyst adds. USD/JPY is up 0.2% at 153.34 and USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,434.45, while AUD/USD is down 0.2% at 0.7038, FactSet data show. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1910 ET - JGB futures edge higher in the morning Tokyo session, tracking overnight price gains in the U.S. Treasury market. The JGB and Treasury markets tend to move in tandem. However, many investors are likely to be in a wait-and-see mode, SMBC Nikko Securities' Lisa Mochizuki says in a research report. JGB market volatility is expected to stay high, at least until Japan's Lower House election on Feb. 8, Mochizuki says. Regarding the Japanese finance ministry's auction today of about 2.8 trillion yen of 2-year sovereign notes, an uneventful result is likely, Mochizuki adds. The 10-year JGB futures are 0.10 yen higher at 131.62 yen. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

1849 ET - Japanese stocks may decline following tech selloffs on Wall Street overnight. Nikkei futures are down 0.2% at 53220 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 152.98, compared with 152.96 as of Thursday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on earnings, with Daiichi Sankyo, Hoya and Komatsu set to report their results. Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's economic plans are also being closely watched ahead of the general election early next month. The Nikkei Stock Average closed flat at 53375.60 on Thursday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1548 ET - Treasury yields decline as November U.S. trade deficit widens more than expected and government officials move to avoid another shutdown. A rally on metals loses steam and the dollar is little changed, following yesterday's expected Fed hold. President Trump says he will announce his Fed pick next week. Markets start to turn their attention to January payrolls --and likely revisions to past data-- due next Friday. December PPI tomorrow is expected to tick higher, in a WSJ consensus. January Chicago business barometer is also forecast to increase slightly. The 10-year falls 0.024 percentage point to 4.226% and the two-year declines 0.029 p.p. to 3.551%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1543 ET - Fixed-income investors should look into non-U.S. government debt for higher return, Wellington Management's Brij Khurana says. Long-term Treasury yields have been elevated due, in part, to worries that Washington will have to issue too much debt to finance a gaping budget deficit. Khurana says some countries with sound credit ratings don't face the same risk and are likely to enter an interest-rate raising cycle while the Fed is easing. He cites Australia and New Zealand among his preferences. He also sees value in inflation-protected bonds and is bracing for a weakening in corporate bonds as companies borrow to finance AI capex. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1540 ET - President Trump says it will reveal his pick to replace Fed Chair Powell next week. If he chooses someone openly supportive of increasing the Fed's balance sheet, markets could sense a risk that the central bank would wind up using the funds to finance government spending, Wellington Management's Brij Khurana says. That would increase inflation risk. "When markets see that the Fed is expanding its balance sheet, they tend to go towards reflation hedges," Khurana says. "They want to hedge the possibility that a larger balance sheet is used for the monetization of debt." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1528 ET - A possible decline in Japanese bond yields could bring down their U.S. equivalent, Wellington Management's Brij Khurana says. Markets have watched yields rise in Japan and feared it could lead local investors to sell off Treasurys in favor of local bonds. But Khurana sees a component of herd behavior that could result in the opposite effect. "As the market reprices term premium everywhere…it kind of affects all the other markets," he says. "If Japanese yields can fall because there is some repatriation of capital, it actually is probably net beneficial to yields in general." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1446 ET - Fair Isaac Corp. Chief Executive William Lansing says credit bureaus are in favor of a tri-merge system (where lenders pull credit data from all three bureaus) rather than a bi-merge system (where lenders can choose just two bureaus to pull from). Lansing acknowledges the tri-merge system gives bureaus a monopoly, but argues that pulling from two bureaus could result in less trustworthy credit data. "You're going to have gaming, you're going to have adverse selection," he says. "There's a cost to be paid for that." FICO, which provides credit scores to bureaus, benefits more from a tri-merge system because it requires more score pulls per lending application.(katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

data.title: Kyivstar Group Ltd. Announces Pricing of Secondary Offering of Common Shares
data.description:
NEW YORK, Jan. 29, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Kyivstar Group Ltd. ; KYIVW) (“Kyivstar” or the “Company”), Ukraine’s leading digital operator, today announced the pricing of the public offering of 12,500,000 common shares (the “Offering”) by VEON Amsterdam B.V., the principal shareholder of the Company, and certain other selling shareholders (collectively, the “Selling Shareholders”) at a public offering price of USD 10.50 per share. The Company is not selling any common shares in the Offering. In connection with the Offering, the Selling Shareholders have granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1,875,000 common shares at the public offering price, less underwriting discounts and commissions.

The Offering is expected to close on February 2, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

Morgan Stanley, Barclays, Cantor and Rothschild & Co are acting as joint booking-running managers and as representatives of the underwriters for the proposed Offering. Benchmark, a StoneX Company, and Northland Capital Markets are acting as co-managers for the proposed Offering.

The Offering is being made only by means of a prospectus. Copies of the prospectus relating to the proposed Offering may be obtained for free by visiting EDGAR on the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission’s (“SEC”) website at www.sec.gov. The prospectus relating to the proposed Offering may also be obtained from:

  • Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC, Attn:Prospectus Department, 180 Varick Street, 2nd Floor, New York, NY 10014;
  • Barclays Capital Inc., c/o Broadridge Financial Solutions, 1155 Long Island Avenue, Edgewood, NY 11717, by telephone at 1-888-603-5847, or by email at barclaysprospectus@broadridge.com;
  • Cantor Fitzgerald & Co., Attention:Capital Markets, 110 East 59th Street, 6th Floor, New York, New York 10022, or by email at prospectus@cantor.com;
  • Rothschild & Co US Inc., 1251 Avenue of the Americas, New York, NY 10020.

A registration statement on Form F-1, including a prospectus, relating to the Offering has been filed on January 28, 2026 with, and was declared effective on January 29, 2026 by, the SEC.

This press release does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy any securities of the Company, and shall not constitute an offer, solicitation or sale in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of that jurisdiction.

About Kyivstar Group Ltd.

Kyivstar Group Ltd. is a Nasdaq-listed holding company that operates JSC Kyivstar, Ukraine’s leading digital operator and the first Ukrainian company to list on a U.S. stock exchange. Kyivstar’s companies provide a broad range of connectivity and digital services, including mobile and fixed-line voice and data, ride-hailing, e-health, digital TV, and enterprise solutions such as Big Data, cloud, and cybersecurity.

Together with VEON, Kyivstar intends to invest USD 1 billion in Ukraine between 2023-2027, through social investments in infrastructure and technological development, charitable donations and strategic acquisitions. For more information, please visit https://investors.kyivstar.ua.

Disclaimer

This press release contains “forward-looking statements,” as the phrase is defined in Section 27A of the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and Section 21E of the U.S. Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. Such forward-looking statements include, but are not limited to, statements relating to, among other things, the timing of the closing of the Offering. There are numerous risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results and performance to differ materially from those expressed by such statements, including risks relating to the timing of the closing of the Offering , among others discussed in the section entitled “Risk Factors” included in the registration statement on Form F-1 filed by Kyivstar with the SEC on January 28, 2026, as amended and supplemented from time to time, and in any other subsequent filings with the SEC by Kyivstar Group. The forward-looking statements contained herein speak only as of the date of this release and Kyivstar disclaims any obligation to update them, except as required by applicable laws.

Contact information

Kyivstar Group Ltd.

Investor Relations

Additional information: pr@kyivstar.net, www.kyivstar.ua.

data.title: London Metal Exchange resumes trade after technical delay
data.description: The London Metal Exchange (LME) resumed trading on Friday following a one-hour delay caused by a technical issue, the exchange said in an earlier emailed notice reviewed by Reuters.

data.title: Yen Set for First Monthly Gain in Five
data.description:
The Japanese yen slipped toward 154 per dollar on Friday but remained on track for its first monthly gain since August, as talks of intervention pushed the currency to four-month highs, even though central bank data suggests authorities have not officially acted.

The yen is up nearly 2% this month and has gained as much as 4.6% from January lows.

The recent rally was initially sparked by reports that the New York Federal Reserve conducted a rate check on the dollar/yen, fueling speculation of a potential joint US-Japan currency intervention.

However, US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent dismissed such reports, reaffirming that the US maintains a strong dollar policy.

Traders, nevertheless, remain cautious about the possibility of unilateral intervention by Japanese authorities, following a series of verbal warnings from local officials.

On the economic front, Japan’s retail sales unexpectedly fell in December, while industrial production declined less than estimated.

data.description:
Lead decreased to 2008.00 USD/T, the lowest since January 2026.

Over the past 4 weeks, Lead lost 0.27%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 2.06%.

data.title: Muzero Acquisition Corp Prices IPO Of 17.50 Mln Units At $10.00/unit
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Muzero Acquisition Corp, a newly organized special purpose acquisition company

formed as a Cayman Islands exempted company, announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 17.50 million units at an offering price of $10.00 per unit.

The company noted that each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one-half of one redeemable warrant. Each whole warrant will entitle the holder thereof to

purchase one Class A ordinary share at $11.50 per share.

The units are expected to trade on the Global Market tier of The Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (Nasdaq) under the ticker symbol MUZEU beginning January 30, 2026. No fractional warrants will be issued upon separation of the units and only whole warrants will trade. Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the ordinary shares and the warrants are expected to be traded on Nasdaq under the symbols MUZE and MUZEW, respectively.

The company has granted the underwriter a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.625 million units at the initial public offering price to cover over-allotments, if any. The offering is expected to close on February 2, 2026,

subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Indonesia May Take More Measures to Improve Transparency in Securities Trading — Market Talk
data.description: Following the recent whipsaw in Indonesian stocks, DBS expects regulators to improve clarity and transparency in securities trading after consulting with key stakeholders. The equity market continued to reverberate on Thursday, continuing a selloff sparked by index provider MSCI Inc.'s warning about Indonesian securities' investability. Regulators unveiled supportive measures Thursday, while state investment agency Danantara recently said it was considering boosting its presence in the capital markets. "We expect more affirmative steps to assuage concerns of the investor community and global index providers, while ironing out opacity of business structure and any remnant governance issues in the medium-term," DBS's Radhika Rao writes in a note. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Union rejects contract offer from Marathon
data.description:
Key points:
  • USW rejects Marathon's contract proposal as "unacceptable"
  • Negotiations face Sunday deadline for new agreement
  • USW demands safety, job security, and fair wages

By Erwin Seba

The United Steelworkers union rejected a comprehensive contract offer from Marathon Petroleum in talks for a national pattern agreement for U.S. refinery and chemical plant workers, the union said on Wednesday night.

The USW and Marathon, lead negotiators for energy companies, face a 12:01 a.m. deadline on Sunday to hammer out a new agreement to the current four-year contract.

The Marathon proposal was "unanimously rejected" by the policy committee, which is made up of members from regions across the U.S. The union did not offer details about the Marathon proposal, but called it "unacceptable."

"MPC continues to meet regularly with representatives from the USW," said Marathon spokesperson Jamal Kheiry. "We are committed to bargaining in good faith and to working toward a mutually satisfactory agreement."

In a statement, the USW said, "we're fighting for safety for all oil workers and our communities, real job security and fair wages that recognize our skill and sacrifices."

data.title: LUV Stock Has Best Day Ever In Its 50-Year History — Morgan Stanley Hears ‘Pre-Pandemic Southwest’ Again
data.description:
Key points:
  • Southwest Airlines forecast fiscal 2026 adjusted profit per share of about $4, compared with Wall Street expectations of $3.45, according to data compiled by Fiscal AI.
  • Morgan Stanley said that the market has noticed the changes at Southwest Airlines, but there is "a lot more" to come.
  • Southwest Airlines’ shares have surged over 17% so far this year and are on course for their third straight year of gains.

Shares of Southwest Airlines surged nearly 19% on Thursday, marking the best day in the U.S. carrier’s 50-year lifetime as a publicly listed company, as a better-than-expected profit forecast for the new year and Wall Street cheer boosted investor sentiment.

Shares of the airline closed at $48.50, their highest since November 2021, after the company outlined efforts to shift its traditional methods and adopt new approaches to drive growth.

LUV’s stock has surged over 17% so far this year and is on course for its third straight year of gains, following a nearly 26% gain in 2025.

The Year Ahead

Southwest on Thursday forecast fiscal 2026 adjusted profit per share of about $4, above Wall Street expectations of $3.45, according to data compiled by Fiscal AI.

The U.S. carrier has been shifting away from its traditional model, implementing bag fees, assigning seats, and extending flight credit expiration. It has also launched free Wi-Fi for loyalty program members in partnership with T-Mobile and expanded its online presence through new partnerships with Expedia and Priceline.

Southwest expects first-quarter unit revenue to increase at least 9.5% and noted that assigned and extra-legroom seating became operational just two days ago. The airline sees earnings upside based on how booking behavior related to those initiatives unfolds.

Wall Street’s View

Morgan Stanley raised the firm's price target on Southwest to $55 from $50 and maintained an ‘Overweight’ rating, according to The Fly. The firm said the airline definitively shed the shackles of strategy and execution risk that have bedeviled the story for much of the post-pandemic era, sounding like the “pre-pandemic Southwest, the industry benchmark.” The firm added that the market has noticed the changes at Southwest Airlines, but there is "a lot more" to come.

Evercore ISI raised its price target on Southwest Airlines to $45 from $40 and kept ‘In Line’ rating, noting that the airline’s earnings slightly exceeded expectations and initial 2026 guidance is "promising," surpassing Street expectations.

What Is Retail Thinking?

Retail sentiment on Southwest Airlines dipped to ‘bearish’ from ‘extremely bullish’ a day ago, with message volumes at ‘extremely high’ levels, according to data from Stocktwits. In the last 24 hours, the retail message volumes on Stocktwits jumped 170%.

A user on Stocktwits noted that Southwest Airlines was 20% away from pre-COVID levels, which is achievable.

https://www.stocktwits.com/R_Stock_Investments/message/643372339

Shares of Southwest Airlines have gained nearly 58% in the last 12 months.

data.title: London Metal Exchange Experiences Trading Glitch
data.description:
(01:54 GMT) *London Metal Exchange: Trading on Its Electronic Trading Platform Is Delayed Due to Technical Issue
(02:05 GMT) London Metal Exchange Experiences Trading Glitch

By Kimberley Kao

The London Metal Exchange delayed the start of trading due to technical issues, it said Friday.

The LME electronic trading platform usually starts trading at 1.00 a.m. London time.

The trading halt came after an intense week for metals, with copper prices hitting record highs on Thursday.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: M EVO GLOBAL ACQUISITION CORP II Prices Upsized IPO Of 27 Mln Units At $10.00/unit
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - M EVO GLOBAL ACQUISITION CORP II announced that it priced its initial public offering of 27 million units at $10.00 per unit after upsizing the original offering of 22.50 million units.

The units will be listed on the Nasdaq Global Market and will trade under the ticker symbol MEVOU beginning January 30, 2026.

The company noted that each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share of the Company, $0.0001 par value per share, and one-half of one redeemable warrant, each whole warrant entitling the holder to purchase one Class A ordinary share upon exercise, at a price of $11.50 per share. Once the securities comprising the units begin separate trading, the Class A ordinary shares and warrants are expected to be listed on the Nasdaq under the symbols MEVOX and MEVOW, respectively.

The offering is expected to close on February 2, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

data.title: Singapore Bank Lending Hits Fresh Record High
data.description:
Singapore's bank loans increased to a new record high of SGD 886.1 billion in December 2025, up from SGD 873.1 billion in the previous month.

Loans to businesses surged to SGD 538.7 billion from SGD 528.3 billion in November, primarily due to increased lending to manufacturing (SGD 27.6 billion vs.

SGD 27.4 billion), agriculture, mining, and quarrying (SGD 3.5 billion vs.

SGD 3.4 billion), general commerce (SGD 99.1 billion vs.

SGD 92 billion), and transportation, storage & communication (SGD 45.8 billion vs.

SGD 44.7 billion).

By contrast, loans to financial and insurance activities fell to SGD 134.5 billion from SGD 136.1 billion.

In addition, consumer loans climbed to SGD 347.4 billion from SGD 344.8 billion, driven mainly by higher housing and bridging loans (SGD 244.1 billion vs.

SGD 242.6 billion), car loans (SGD 9.3 billion vs.

SGD 9.1 billion), credit card loans (SGD 17.8 billion vs.

SGD 17.6 billion), and other personal loans (SGD 75.6 billion vs.

SGD 74.8 billion).

data.title: Gold slips on firmer dollar, but set for best month since 1980
data.description:
Gold prices fell on Friday, pressured by a firmer dollar, but were set for their biggest monthly gain since 1980 as investors piled into the safe-haven metal amid persistent geopolitical and economic uncertainties.

FUNDAMENTALS

* Spot gold was down 0.9% at $5,346.42 per ounce, as of 0124 GMT, after scaling a record $5,594.82 the previous day.

* Prices have risen more than 24% so far in January, heading for a sixth straight month of gains and the largest monthly advance since January 1980.

* U.S. gold futures for February delivery climbed 1.3% to $5,390.80 per ounce on Friday.

* Spot silver was down 0.2% at $115.83 an ounce, after hitting a record high of $121.64 on Thursday. The metal has gained 62% so far this month, on track for its best-ever monthly performance.

* The dollar index edged higher, supported in part by the Federal Reserve's decision on Wednesday to leave interest rates unchanged, but was poised for a second straight weekly decline.

* Fed Chair Jerome Powell said inflation in December was likely well above the central bank's 2% target.

* U.S. economic data on Thursday showed weekly initial jobless claims fell, indicating layoffs remained low, although soft hiring kept consumers pessimistic about the labour market.

* U.S. President Donald Trump is weighing options against Iran that include targeted strikes on security forces and leaders to inspire protesters, multiple sources said, even as Israeli and Arab officials said air power alone would not topple the clerical rulers.

* Gold exports from Switzerland rose 27% month-on-month in December as shipments to Britain jumped to their highest since August 2019, Swiss customs data showed on Thursday.

* UBS raised its gold price target to $6,200 per ounce for March, June and September 2026, compared with a prior forecast of $5,000, citing stronger-than-expected demand stemming from increased investment.

* Spot platinum lost 0.9% to $2,606.15 per ounce after hitting a record $2,918.80 on Monday, while palladium rose 0.5% to $2,016.69.

DATA/EVENTS (GMT)

1330

US PPI Machine Manuf'ing Dec

data.title: Key facts: Wells Fargo, RBC raise ASML price targets; bookings show mixed signals
data.description:
• Wells Fargo has increased its price target for ASML Holding shares to $1650.00 from $1450.00, while RBC Capital has also raised its price target to $1625.00 per share, up from $1550.00.1
  • ASML's guidance shows a year-over-year drop in bookings exceeding 10%. However, Barclays noted that bookings from China matched previous record quarters, indicating demand may not be as weak.2
  • ASML Holding (ASML) experienced a rise in share value as part of the semiconductor sector's positive performance, following favorable earnings reports from STMicro.3

data.title: Key facts: Tom Hayes sues UBS for $400M; UBS boosts Ping An stake
data.description:
• Tom Hayes has filed a lawsuit against UBS Group in the U.S., claiming over $400 million in damages for being labeled the 'evil mastermind' of the Libor rate-rigging scandal.1
  • UBS Group AG's long position in H shares of Ping An Insurance increased to 9.39% on January 23, up from 8.83%, while its short position in the same shares rose to 7.96%, up from 6.93%.2
  • On January 29, 2026, UBS Group AG disclosed it reduced its voting rights in Solvay SA to below 3% on January 26, 2026, due to lower indirect holdings.3

data.title: Key facts: Novo Nordisk's Wegovy approved; ad budget to hit $500M; lawsuit filed
data.description:
• Novo Nordisk's Wegovy® pill, the first oral GLP-1 weight loss drug, has gained FDA approval, while BetterWay launches a GLP-1 blood test panel at 25+ locations in Texas.1
  • Novo Nordisk plans to boost its U.S. ad budget for Wegovy and Ozempic to nearly $500 million in 2025, more than double Eli Lilly's spending, with a 50% increase from last year.2
  • A class action lawsuit against Novo Nordisk claims the company delayed generic versions of its diabetes drug Victoza through a 2019 settlement, raising legal and financial concerns.3

data.title: Key facts: Mercedes-Benz CEO warns EU targets may hinder EV shift; Lutnick suggests US headquarters move
data.description:
• Mercedes-Benz CEO Ola Kaellenius warned that the EU's relaxed electric vehicle targets could harm the shift to electric cars and lead to market shrinkage, ahead of the new S-class launch.1
  • Mercedes-Benz's CEO announced that Howard Lutnick, a notable finance industry leader, has urged the company to consider moving its headquarters to the United States.2

data.title: Key facts: TotalEnergies restarts $20B Mozambique LNG project; shares rise 1.7%
data.description:
• TotalEnergies has restarted its $20 billion LNG project in Mozambique, with cost talks resuming. The project aims to deliver LNG by 2029, with increased activity expected soon.12
  • TotalEnergies gained 1.7% as European energy stocks rose, driven by increasing oil prices that reached their highest level since late September.3

data.title: Key facts: Sibanye Stillwater advances Burnstone project; targets 3B rand savings
data.description:
• Sibanye Stillwater is progressing its Burnstone gold project, with a feasibility study and final investment decision expected in early 2026, driven by rising gold prices.1
  • Sibanye-Stillwater aims to save 3 billion rand by 2027, cut gross debt by 50% from $2.2 billion, and boost production by 2.5% in gold equivalent ounces.2

data.title: Key facts: Sanofi announces EUR 1B buyback; Q4 sales rise 13%; ten approvals
data.description:
• Sanofi plans a EUR 1 billion share buyback this year and another EUR 1 billion in 2026. Q4 sales reached EUR 11.3 billion, a 13% rise at constant currency.12
  • Sanofi gained ten regulatory approvals in various areas, reported positive phase 3 results for amlitelimab in atopic dermatitis and Dupixent, but tolebrutinib failed to meet its primary endpoint.34
  • Sanofi expects sales to grow by a high-single-digit percentage in 2026, driven by strong demand for its asthma drug Dupixent and newer medicines.5
